The Spear Cuts Through Water.blurb: A genre-bending fantasy epic told through nested narratives—a theater performance, ancestral memory, and direct address—following two warriors escorting a dying god-empress across a tyrannical empire. Stunning prose experimentation for readers who value narrative innovation and emotional resonance over conventional plotting.
The Spear Cuts Through Water.keywords: literary fantasy, experimental narrative, second-person pov, metafiction, queer protagonists, tyranny and revolution, lyrical prose, nested stories, philippine-inspired, memory and trauma

The Wounded Kingdom.blurb: Conclusion to Barker's Wounded Kingdom trilogy featuring an assassin protagonist in a world of bone ships and giant sea creatures, praised for its inventive magic system involving 'guilt-magic' and disability representation woven organically into both character and worldbuilding.
The Wounded Kingdom.keywords: epic fantasy, assassin protagonist, disability representation, bone magic, naval warfare, political intrigue, darker tone, unique worldbuilding, trilogy conclusion, character-driven

The Court of Broken Knives.blurb: Brutal, poetic grimdark following a band of mercenaries tasked with killing a god-emperor — distinctive for its lyrical, fragmented prose and unflinching nihilism that divides readers sharply.
The Court of Broken Knives.keywords: grimdark, epic fantasy, mercenaries, poetic prose, violent, nihilistic, fragmented style, anti-hero, god-emperor, morally dark, visceral

Kings of the Wyld.blurb: Aging rock-star mercenaries reunite for one last quest to save a bandmate's daughter, blending D&D tropes with irreverent humor and genuine heart. A fun, self-aware romp that trades depth for pure entertainment and nostalgic camaraderie.
Kings of the Wyld.keywords: epic fantasy, comedy, band of brothers, rock star metaphor, aging heroes, monster fights, road journey, adventure, heartfelt, nostalgia

And What Can We Offer You Tonight.blurb: A novella about sentient sex workers in a fantastical brothel who shape-shift to fulfill clients' desires, exploring bodily autonomy and resistance through lyrical, evocative prose.
And What Can We Offer You Tonight.keywords: novella, literary fantasy, sex work, body horror, shape-shifting, autonomy, resistance, lush prose, feminist, speculative, dark

Mothtown.blurb: An atmospheric, surreal literary mystery about a man obsessed with mysterious moths in a slowly disintegrating world where ecological collapse blurs into dreamlike horror. Trades conventional plot momentum for mood, ambiguity, and unsettling metaphor.
Mothtown.keywords: speculative fiction, literary, climate fiction, surreal, atmospheric, ecological collapse, slow-burn, ambiguous, moths, dreamlike, psychological, british, melancholic

Lungfish.blurb: A character-driven literary debut following a reclusive woman in rural Maine whose solitary existence is disrupted when she becomes entangled with her neighbors, exploring themes of isolation, connection, and the weight of the past through quiet, observant prose.
Lungfish.keywords: literary fiction, rural maine, isolation, character study, quiet, introspective, contemporary, atmospheric, solitude, human connection

The Wickwire Watch.blurb: A clockmaker's apprentice discovers their craft intersects with deeper metaphysical machinery in this introspective fantasy that prioritizes character interiority and lyrical prose over spectacle.
The Wickwire Watch.keywords: literary fantasy, clockwork magic, introspective, character-driven, lyrical prose, metaphysical, coming-of-age, philosophical, slow-burn

12 Miles Below.blurb: Post-apocalyptic science fantasy following a scavenger exploring a frozen surface world where humanity survives in underground kingdoms, blending mech suits, ancient AI, and detailed worldbuilding with progression elements and strong voice-driven narration.
12 Miles Below.keywords: science fantasy, post-apocalyptic, progression, worldbuilding-heavy, mechs, ai companions, frozen wasteland, scavenger protagonist, litrpg-adjacent, voice-driven

The Calamitous Bob.blurb: A competent epic fantasy by the Mass Effect and Star Wars video game writer that delivers straightforward adventure with solid action sequences but struggles to distinguish itself in a crowded genre.
The Calamitous Bob.keywords: epic fantasy, adventure, action-focused, straightforward plotting, video game writer, secondary world, traditional structure, accessible

Little Blue Encyclopedia.blurb: An unconventional fictional encyclopedia about a beloved trans actress from an imagined TV show, told through the grief-stricken lens of a trans woman mourning both the actress and their own unrealized life. Structurally inventive and emotionally raw, it explores parasocial relationships, trans experience, and the ways media shapes identity.
Little Blue Encyclopedia.keywords: literary fiction, experimental structure, trans protagonist, grief, encyclopedia format, parasocial relationships, character study, queer lit, emotional, metafictional

Dune: Messiah.blurb: A deliberately anti-heroic sequel that deconstructs the messianic triumph of Dune, trading desert adventure for a claustrophobic court tragedy about the horrors of prescience and power. Suits readers who want ideas, irony, and tragedy over spectacle.
Dune: Messiah.keywords: soft sci-fi, political intrigue, tragedy, prescience, deconstruction, philosophical, messianic, cerebral, slow-burn

Of Blood and Fire.blurb: A comfort-food debut epic fantasy leaning hard into classic tropes — dragons, a chosen-one farm boy, and an ancient evil reawakening — executed with earnest momentum rather than novelty. Best for readers who want familiar Eragon/Wheel-of-Time beats done with sincere enthusiasm.
Of Blood and Fire.keywords: epic fantasy, dragons, chosen one, coming-of-age, tropey, classic high fantasy, ancient evil, fast-paced, debut

The Fall.blurb: A prequel novella/short to the Bound and the Broken saga depicting the fall of the dragon-bonded order — fast, action-forward, and emotionally charged backstory that rewards fans of the main series more than newcomers.
The Fall.keywords: epic fantasy, dragons, prequel, tragic, action-driven, bonded companions, secondary world, classic-flavored

The Chain and the Flame.blurb: A later entry in Cahill's Bound and the Broken saga, escalating the dragon-bonded epic with bigger battles and rising stakes; suits readers who love classic, tropey, emotionally earnest epic fantasy in the vein of Eragon and Wheel of Time.
The Chain and the Flame.keywords: epic fantasy, dragons, multi-pov, coming-of-age, war, magic system, classic tropes, earnest, high-stakes, secondary world

A Storm of Mist and Thunder.blurb: A later entry in Cahill's Bound and the Broken saga, leaning hard into dragon-bonded warriors, escalating battles, and high-emotion epic set-pieces — comfort-food classic fantasy for readers who want Eragon-meets-Wheel-of-Time spectacle and don't mind familiar tropes.
A Storm of Mist and Thunder.keywords: epic fantasy, dragons, multi-pov, classic-fantasy, battle-heavy, emotional, coming-of-age, secondary world, magic system

The War of Seven Nights.blurb: A short companion novella in Cahill's Bound and the Broken world, delivering a tightly-focused war-siege story for fans craving more dragons and elven lore between the main doorstopper installments.
The War of Seven Nights.keywords: epic fantasy, novella, dragons, war, siege, secondary world, companion story, action-driven

The Republic of Thieves.blurb: The long-awaited third Gentleman Bastard book finally delivers Sabetha, weaving a rival-mages election scheme in the present with a flashback theatrical caper, but it's more a character-and-relationship piece than a tight heist. Best for readers invested in Locke and Jean who want banter, romance, and witty prose over momentum.
The Republic of Thieves.keywords: fantasy, heist, con artists, witty banter, dual timeline, romance, magic, secondary world, voice-driven

Of Darkness and Light.blurb: The second volume in The Bound and the Broken, escalating its dragon-bonded, Tolkien-and-Sapkowski-inspired epic with bigger battles and deepening companionship; suits readers who want classic comfort-food epic fantasy with dragons and emotional payoffs over genre-bending novelty.
Of Darkness and Light.keywords: epic fantasy, dragons, multi-pov, coming-of-age, battle, chosen-one, classic-flavored, companionship, escalating stakes

The War of Broken Saints.blurb: A later entry in Cahill's Bound and the Broken saga that doubles down on large-scale dragon-riding warfare and ensemble payoffs; suited to readers who want sweeping, emotionally-driven, Eragon-meets-Wheel-of-Time epic fantasy comfort reading over literary subtlety.
The War of Broken Saints.keywords: epic fantasy, dragons, multi-pov, coming-of-age, war, magic system, secondary world, emotional, classic-flavored

A Shadow of Light.blurb: The second Bound and the Broken installment ramps up the dragon-bonded coming-of-age action and expands the war against the Lorian empire; a comfort-read for fans of classic Eragon/Wheel of Time tropes who want epic battles and emotional stakes over subversion.
A Shadow of Light.keywords: epic fantasy, dragons, coming-of-age, multi-pov, war, magic system, tropey, heroic, second-book, fast-paced

The Bound and the Broken.blurb: A heartfelt, dragon-soaked debut epic fantasy that wears its Eragon and Stormlight influences proudly, trading novelty for emotional sincerity and crowd-pleasing battle setpieces. Suits readers who want comfort-food classic epic fantasy with bonded dragons and a clear good-vs-evil arc.
The Bound and the Broken.keywords: epic fantasy, dragons, coming-of-age, multi-pov, classic fantasy, war, emotional, action-driven, derivative-but-cozy

Of War and Ruin.blurb: The fourth installment of The Bound and the Broken delivers escalating dragon-bonded warfare and big emotional setpieces for readers who love classic, tropey, high-stakes epic fantasy in the Eragon-meets-Wheel-of-Time vein. Best for fans craving comfort-food fantasy with dragons, found family, and large-scale battles over genre-bending innovation.
Of War and Ruin.keywords: epic fantasy, dragons, multi-pov, found family, battle-heavy, magic system, tropey, high-stakes, war, coming-of-age

Of Empires and Dust.blurb: The fourth Bound and Broken installment ramps the dragon-bonded war epic into widescreen multi-POV setpieces, leaning hard into emotional gut-punches and large-scale battle spectacle for readers who love Eragon-meets-Wheel-of-Time comfort epic fantasy.
Of Empires and Dust.keywords: epic fantasy, dragons, multi-pov, war, coming-of-age, magic system, battle spectacle, emotional, derivative-but-fun

Of Gods and Ashes.blurb: A companion entry in Cahill's Bound and Broken universe that delivers dragon-fueled, emotionally charged epic fantasy battles; suited to readers who want classic heroic adventure and visceral action over genre reinvention.
Of Gods and Ashes.keywords: epic fantasy, dragons, action-heavy, heroic, secondary world, emotional, battle-focused, classic-tropes, fast-paced

Katabasis.blurb: Two rival Cambridge magick PhD students descend into a hell built from the eight courts of philosophy to retrieve their dead advisor's soul — a dark academia journey that weaponizes logic, grief, and academic toxicity. Suits readers who like idea-dense, footnote-flavored fantasy and won't mind that the underworld doubles as a thesis on knowledge and ambition.
Katabasis.keywords: dark academia, underworld journey, magick system, philosophical, grief, academic satire, enemies to lovers, rivals, cerebral, literary fantasy

Yellowface.blurb: A propulsive, darkly comic first-person thriller about a white author stealing her dead Asian friend's manuscript, skewering publishing's race politics and social-media pile-ons. Suits readers who want a fast, uncomfortable, satirical page-turner over literary subtlety.
Yellowface.keywords: literary fiction, satire, unreliable narrator, publishing industry, dark comedy, social media, race, thriller-paced, voice-driven

The Buried Giant.blurb: A spare, melancholic post-Arthurian fable about an elderly couple journeying across a mist-shrouded Britain where collective forgetting buries old wartime atrocities; suits readers who want allegory and meditation on memory and grief over fantasy adventure.
The Buried Giant.keywords: literary fantasy, allegory, post-arthurian, memory, melancholy, slow-paced, elegiac, fable, quest

Little, Big.blurb: A sprawling, dreamlike multigenerational saga where a family's encounters with faerie unfold as a 'Tale' larger than any of them; ravishing prose and mythic structure for patient readers who prize atmosphere and meaning over momentum.
Little, Big.keywords: literary fantasy, faerie, multigenerational, dreamlike, lyrical prose, mythic, slow-burn, atmospheric, americana, melancholy

Soldier of the Mist.blurb: A mercenary in ancient Greece who forgets everything each day but can see gods writes his life on a scroll — a puzzle-box narrative demanding active reconstruction, suited to patient readers who relish ambiguity and mythological texture over conventional plot momentum.
Soldier of the Mist.keywords: literary fantasy, ancient greece, unreliable narrator, mythological, amnesia, puzzle-box, gods, dense, slow-burn
